pattern: Assigning each node the heap-style index of its position in a complete tree turns level width into the difference between the first and last indices on that level.
hints: Width counts the null gaps between the outermost real nodes, as if the level were part of a perfect tree.
If you label a node with index i, its children naturally take indices 2*i and 2*i+1, mirroring an array-backed complete tree.
Process level by level tracking each node's index; the level's width is lastIndex minus firstIndex plus one, and you keep the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: width comes from a level with gaps
two nodes at the second level
perfect tree, widest at the bottom

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes, bounded node count
stress.note: node values irrelevant to width; shape varies
